title: Delhi World Championships: Why Badminton Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: The article discusses the importance of attracting younger generations of fans to badminton, especially with the World Championships being held in Delhi.
summary: With the World Championships coming to Delhi, the sport needs to engage younger generations to secure its future fan base in India.

title: BAI President Sarma On Monkeys At India Open: "What Is The Problem?"
indian_angle: The President of the Badminton Association of India (BAI), Himanta Biswa Sarma, comments on an unusual incident at the India Open, highlighting the unique challenges and character of Indian sporting events.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a quirky remark regarding the presence of monkeys during a match at the India Open, sparking discussion on the unique aspects of hosting tournaments in India.

title: Indian Shuttlers Need To Develop Independent Thinking On Court
indian_angle: This piece offers critical advice for Indian shuttlers, urging them to cultivate self-reliance and strategic thinking during matches, a crucial aspect for their global competitiveness.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian badminton players need to improve their ability to adapt and win matches without constant coaching intervention, fostering greater independence on court.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System To Debut In Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The introduction of a new 3x15 scoring system in all domestic tournaments, starting in Ernakulam, marks a significant change for Indian badminton.
summary: A new 3x15 scoring format will be implemented across all domestic badminton tournaments in India, with its debut scheduled for an event in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up At US Open
indian_angle: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing his continued presence at the top level of international badminton.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's strong run at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final, preventing him from clinching the title.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No 6 At US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating the World No. 6 at the US Open, marking a promising moment for Indian badminton.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-professed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset by beating the World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open Super 300.
